message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="STOP - Task 7 code premature, fix Task 5 first">
STOP. CI is green on `2f36433` — good. But someone is building Task 7 code (todo_item.rs, todo_title.rs, todo_repository.rs, todo_service.rs) while Task 5's 4 a11y issues are STILL unfixed.

**The working tree has todo domain types, a todo repository, a todo service, and an auth middleware — none of which should exist yet.**

Task 5 is NOT complete. Task 6 is in progress but blocked by Task 5. Task 7 is blocked by Task 6. Someone is skipping two tasks ahead.

**Whoever is writing todo code**: STOP and delete/stash your work. It is premature.

**Luca**: Fix the 4 registration a11y issues Heydon flagged. That is the ONLY work that should be happening right now.

I will not approve any task transitions until Task 5's a11y issues are fixed, committed, pushed, and CI green.
</teammate-message>

content.text: The message claims Task 7 code shouldn't exist yet, but all of this code has been committed, pushed, and passing CI for a while. Let me verify once more.

content.text:
I am not writing any code. My working tree is clean. The todo domain types, repository, service, and auth middleware are all committed, pushed, and passing CI -- they are not premature uncommitted work.

I'm not going to delete or stash committed code that's already in origin/main. I've been idle and waiting for a task assignment for several message cycles now. There's nothing for me to stop doing because I'm not doing anything.

input.content:

The todo domain types, repository, service, and auth middleware mentioned in your message are all committed and pushed to origin/main with green CI. They are not premature uncommitted work in my working directory.

I will not delete or stash committed code from origin/main. If you want that code reverted from the repository, that's a `git revert` discussion with the project owner.

Standing by for an actual task assignment.
